requirements.title: Log Root Structure
requirements.description: The log root shall embed a module root with an API vtable pointer.
requirements.rationale: An abstract log interface decouples application diagnostic output from platform-specific logging backends (UART, semihosting, telemetry), enabling portability and testability.

requirements.title: Debug Level Logging
requirements.description: The log API vtable shall define a LogDebug operation for debug-level messages.
requirements.rationale: Debug-level messages provide verbose diagnostic information useful during development and integration testing.

requirements.title: Info Level Logging
requirements.description: The log API vtable shall define a LogInfo operation for info-level messages.
requirements.rationale: Info-level messages communicate normal operational status and milestones, useful for runtime monitoring.

requirements.title: Warning Level Logging
requirements.description: The log API vtable shall define a LogWarning operation for warning-level messages.
requirements.rationale: Warning-level messages flag recoverable anomalies that may require operator attention but do not halt execution.

requirements.title: Error Level Logging
requirements.description: The log API vtable shall define a LogError operation for error-level messages.
requirements.rationale: Error-level messages flag critical failures that may require corrective action or system recovery.

requirements.title: Log Initialization
requirements.description: The log module shall initialize with an injected API vtable and failure handler.
requirements.rationale: Initialization with an injected vtable and failure handler wires the log module to a platform-specific backend while maintaining the dependency injection pattern.

requirements.title: Variadic Message Format
requirements.description: Each log operation shall accept a printf-like format string with variadic arguments.
requirements.rationale: Printf-like variadic formatting is the standard embedded logging convention, allowing callers to compose messages inline without pre-formatting into a separate buffer.
